seoTitle: How to Wrap Text in Microsoft Excel Cells
metaDescription: Fix overflowing or clipped text in Excel by learning the one-click wrap text shortcut, manual line breaks, and row height adjustments that keep tables tidy.
intro: Long notes, product descriptions, or tracking strings that overflow into blank cells or get clipped out of view make a spreadsheet hard to read. Excel's text wrap feature keeps everything visible inside the cell without forcing you to widen columns. This guide covers the ribbon shortcut plus manual line breaks for finer control.

steps.heading: Select the cells with long text
steps.body: Highlight the cell or range of cells containing the text that's overflowing or getting cut off before applying any formatting.

steps.heading: Apply Wrap Text from the Home ribbon
steps.body: Click the Wrap Text button in the Home tab's alignment group to instantly wrap the content within the existing column width, keeping all typed content visible.

steps.heading: Insert manual line breaks for precise control
steps.body: For more control over exactly where a line splits, place your cursor in the formula bar at the desired spot and insert a manual line break rather than relying on automatic wrapping alone.

steps.heading: Adjust row height if text is still clipped
steps.body: If wrapped text is still cut off vertically, adjust the row height manually or use auto-fit to expand the row until every line is visible.

steps.heading: Fine-tune alignment for a clean look
steps.body: Adjust vertical and horizontal alignment settings after wrapping to keep multi-line cells looking neat and consistent across your table.

faq.question: Does wrapping text change my column width?
faq.answer: No, wrap text keeps the column width the same and instead increases the row height so the full text remains visible.

faq.question: Can I control exactly where the line breaks?
faq.answer: Yes, by inserting a manual line break inside the formula bar you can force the text to split at a specific point rather than letting Excel wrap it automatically.

faq.question: Why isn't my text wrapping even after I click Wrap Text?
faq.answer: This is often caused by a fixed row height, so try auto-fitting the row height after applying wrap text.

faq.question: Will wrapped text print correctly?
faq.answer: Yes, as long as the row height is properly adjusted, wrapped text prints the same as it appears on screen.
